hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_title: First Impressions in Milan -- Introducing Yourself and Your Organization
lesson.menu_desc: Make a strong first impression at an international event: introduce yourself and describe your organization in English, with no translator -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 16 -- First Impressions in Milan
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 16 -- First Impressions in Milan
lesson.grammar_point: formal introductions and describing your organization (relative clauses as chunk)

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
an organization created for a public purpose
b
a person who speaks or acts for an organization
c
the full range of programs or products an organization offers
d
between two countries, groups, or sides
e
the formal rules and steps people follow
f
an official connection to an organization
g
a group of people who represent a country or group
h
the main office of an organization
i
a part of the economy or area of business
j
the opinion people have about someone or something

paras: Flavia is at an international business event in Milan. This time there is no translator, so she introduces herself in English. She takes a short breath and smiles. 'Good morning. My name is Flavia. I am a representative of Sebrae, which is an institution that supports small businesses in Brazil.' A clear first impression opens every door.

Then she describes her organization. 'Sebrae is an institution that helps small companies grow. Our headquarters is in Brazil, but we work in many countries.' She talks about the portfolio of programs that Sebrae offers, and about the sector where most of her clients work. She also mentions the strong reputation that Sebrae has with international partners.

A man from an Italian delegation comes to talk. He asks about a possible affiliation between the two groups. Flavia explains the protocol for a bilateral project -- the formal steps that both sides follow. 'It is a pleasure to meet a partner who shares our vision,' she says. Her calm, formal introduction makes a strong first impression, and a new bilateral connection begins.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about a formal self-introduction and describing your organization at an international event

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia cannot speak without a translator, so she stays quiet at the event.
Flavia introduces herself and her organization in English and makes a strong first impression.
The text explains how to book a hotel room in Milan.

gapfill.text: Introduce yourself and your organization with three relative-clause chunks: 'I am a representative WHO leads missions', 'Sebrae is an institution THAT supports small businesses', and 'Milan is the city WHERE the event takes place'. Use who for people, that/which for organizations, and where for places.

bank.label: Useful sentences to introduce yourself and your organization
items: Good morning. My name is Flavia, and I am a representative of Sebrae.
Sebrae is an institution that supports small businesses in Brazil.
Our headquarters is in Brazil, but we work in many countries.
We offer a portfolio of programs for small companies.
It is a pleasure to meet a partner who shares our vision.
I would like to talk about a possible affiliation between our groups.
This is the protocol that both sides follow for a bilateral project.
Sebrae has a strong reputation in this sector.

lf.title: Complete the introduction
items: It is a pleasure to meet a partner

lf.text: Use who for people, that or which for organizations and things, and where for places. These short relative chunks add information to your introduction in a natural, formal way.

items.situation: You meet someone new at an international event. Introduce yourself and your role.
tips: Good morning. My name is Flavia, and I am a representative of Sebrae.
Hello. I am Flavia. I represent Sebrae, an institution that supports small businesses.

items.situation: The person asks what your organization does. Describe it in one sentence.
tips: Sebrae is an institution that supports small businesses in Brazil.
We are an institution that helps small companies grow.

items.situation: You want to talk about the programs your organization offers.
tips: We offer a portfolio of programs for small companies.
Our portfolio includes training and support for the small-business sector.

items.situation: A delegation asks about working together. Suggest a next step politely.
tips: I would like to talk about a possible affiliation between our groups.
Let me explain the protocol for a bilateral project.

items: Introduce yourself at the event. Say your name, your role, and one relative clause about Sebrae. Use 'I am a representative of...' and 'an institution that...'.
Describe your organization: its headquarters, its portfolio, and its reputation. Use two relative clauses (that / where).
Give a full introduction: who you are, what Sebrae is, and one thing your organization offers -- no notes.
A delegation from Italy asks about an affiliation. Introduce yourself, describe Sebrae, and suggest the protocol for a bilateral project.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Introduce yourself: 'Good morning. My name is Flavia, and I am a representative of Sebrae.'
Describe the organization: 'Sebrae is an institution that supports small businesses in Brazil.'
Add detail with a relative clause: 'We offer a portfolio of programs that helps small companies grow.'
Open a connection: 'It is a pleasure to meet a partner who shares our vision.'
answerkey.note: A calm, formal introduction with clear relative clauses makes a strong first impression -- no translator needed.

listenings.text: Good morning. My name is Flavia, and it is a pleasure to be here in Milan. I am a representative of Sebrae, which is an institution that supports small businesses in Brazil. Our headquarters is in Brazil, but we work with partners in many countries. We offer a portfolio of programs that helps small companies grow, and Sebrae has a strong reputation in this sector. I am here to meet partners who share our vision, and to talk about a possible affiliation between our groups. Thank you very much.

Good morning. Here is one simple tip for a strong first impression at an international event. First, keep your introduction short and clear. Say your name and your role: I am a representative of my organization. Second, describe your organization in one sentence. Use a relative clause: we are an institution that supports small businesses. Third, open a connection. Say, it is a pleasure to meet a partner who shares our vision. A calm, formal introduction opens every door. Good luck!

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia introduces herself and says, my name is Flavia, and I am a representative of Sebrae. Then, she describes her organization and says, Sebrae is an institution that supports small businesses. Next, she talks about the portfolio of programs that Sebrae offers. After that, a man from an Italian delegation asks about a possible affiliation. Finally, she explains the protocol for a bilateral project and opens a new connection.
